The closest defensible FoodOn anchor is the generic confection class. A more specific URI should be assigned when FoodOn expands Southeast Asian kueh coverage.
ontology.foodex2_description: Sweet snacks and confectionery — used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for traditional coconut-based kueh platter; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for Kueh Dadar, Ondeh Ondeh, or Bingka Ubi.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not contain a specific code for Singaporean kueh. A0CKL (sweet snacks and confectionery) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for this traditional Singaporean kueh platter.

serving.description: 1 platter (3 pieces: 1 Kueh Dadar roll, 2 Ondeh Ondeh balls, 1 slice Bingka Ubi)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of the Coconut Oasis platter is approximately 120 g, comprising one pandan-wrapped Kueh Dadar roll, two Ondeh Ondeh glutinous rice balls filled with gula melaka, and one slice of Bingka Ubi tapioca cake.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Kueh Dadar, Ondeh Ondeh, Bingka Ubi composite; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for coconut milk, glutinous rice flour, and gula melaka components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The input record cr_id G00011051 carries HPB provenance with confidence 0.95. The per_100g values (energy 895 kcal, fat 99.1 g, carbohydrate 0.84 g, protein 0.0 g) are inconsistent with a mixed kueh platter and are more consistent with a near-pure fat ingredient such as coconut oil or coconut cream concentrate. The HPB source values are preserved as supplied because the record is marked reviewed and canonical with 0.95 confidence. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 86.5 g/100g based on the known fatty acid profile of coconut oil/cream (USDA FoodData Central NDB 04047, Coconut oil). Monounsaturated fat approximately 6.0 g/100g and polyunsaturated fat approximately 1.8 g/100g are estimated from the same USDA reference. A flag is raised in notes that the macro profile does not match a typical kueh platter and may represent a coconut oil or coconut cream ingredient mislabelled as a dish.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Per the per_100g data as supplied by HPB (carbohydrate 0.84 g/100g), this record has negligible carbohydrate content. Glycemic index is not applicable for foods with negligible carbohydrates. glycemic_index is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel consistent with not_applicable gi_category (not a true GI value). Glycemic load per serving is 0.0. Note: if this record is corrected to reflect a true kueh platter, GI should be re-evaluated against the University of Sydney GI Database for glutinous rice-based confections.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high saturated fat content (~86.5 g/100g) contributing to arterial stiffness and cardiovascular risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this food; the saturated fat content far exceeds the AHA recommended threshold of 5–6% of total daily calories from saturated fat, and high saturated fat intake is associated with increased arterial stiffness and elevated LDL cholesterol, both of which worsen blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Dominant saturated fat (~86.5 g/100g) impairing insulin sensitivity; negligible carbohydrate does not offset the metabolic risk
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of this food; while carbohydrate and sugar content are negligible and will not cause acute postprandial glucose spikes, the extremely high saturated fat load impairs insulin receptor sensitivity and is incompatible with ADA dietary guidelines for T2DM management, which recommend replacing saturated fat with monounsaturated or polyunsaturated fats.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ~86.5 g/100g raises LDL cholesterol and increases atherosclerotic plaque risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this food; the saturated fat content of approximately 86.5 g per 100 g is among the highest of any food category and is strongly associated with elevated LDL-C, reduced HDL-C, and accelerated atherosclerosis per ACC/AHA cardiovascular risk guidelines; substitution with extra-virgin olive oil or avocado oil is recommended for cooking.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Dominant saturated fat triggers pro-inflammatory cytokines TNF-alpha and IL-6
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food; the saturated fat profile (~86.5 g/100g) is a hard disqualifier under anti-inflammatory dietary frameworks, as high saturated fat intake upregulates TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines; replace with extra-virgin olive oil which provides anti-inflammatory oleocanthal and oleic acid.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coconut Oasis is a traditional Singaporean kueh platter offered as a set comprising three classic Peranakan and Malay confections: Kueh Dadar (a pandan-flavoured crepe rolled around sweetened grated coconut), Ondeh Ondeh (small glutinous rice flour balls filled with liquid gula melaka palm sugar and coated in freshly grated coconut), and Bingka Ubi (a baked or steamed tapioca cake made with coconut milk, eggs, and sugar). The platter celebrates the dominant flavour trio of Southeast Asian kueh: pandan, coconut, and gula melaka. Texturally, Kueh Dadar is soft and chewy with a fragrant pandan aroma; Ondeh Ondeh delivers a burst of warm palm sugar syrup when bitten; and Bingka Ubi is dense, moist, and slightly caramelised on the surface. From a nutritional standpoint, the per_100g data as supplied by HPB (energy 895 kcal, fat 99.1 g, carbohydrate 0.84 g, protein 0.0 g) is atypical for a mixed kueh platter and is more consistent with a concentrated coconut fat ingredient such as coconut oil or coconut cream. This anomaly is flagged for review. If the values are taken at face value, the food is extremely energy-dense and dominated by saturated fat (~86.5 g/100g estimated), making it clinically unsuitable for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Protein is naturally zero as reported by HPB for this record. Carbohydrate is negligible at 0.84 g/100g per the source, making glycemic index not applicable. Sodium is confirmed zero per HPB. Dietary fibre is confirmed zero per HPB. A typical serving of the platter is approximately 120 g.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Kueh Dadar?
Is Coconut Oasis suitable for diabetics?

Ondeh Ondeh: glutinous rice ball with palm sugar
